ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hardware_components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware component */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the hardware component */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the hardware component */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device the hardware component is used for */,
  "manufacturing_date" TEXT /* Date the hardware component was manufactured */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the hardware component */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_fields" (
  "field_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration field */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated hardware component */,
  "field_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration field */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the configuration field */,
  "read_write" TEXT /* Access type (Read Only, Read/Write) */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* Default reset value for the configuration field */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of parameter being configured */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "valid_range" TEXT /* Valid range of values for the configuration field */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("field_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_fields_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES hardware_components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the user role */,
  "permissions_json" TEXT /* JSON representation of the permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role of the user in the system */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change record */,
  "field_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration field that was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the change was made */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made */,
  "change_comment" TEXT /* Comment provided by the user for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_changes_field_id FOREIGN KEY ("field_id") REFERENCES configuration_fields ("field_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_changes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the event */,
  "event" TEXT /* Description of the event that occurred */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event was logged */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log */,
  "event_type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the event_types table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_event_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_type_id") REFERENCES event_types ("event_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reset_values" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset value record */,
  "field_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration field */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* The reset value for the configuration field */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reset_values_field_id FOREIGN KEY ("field_id") REFERENCES configuration_fields ("field_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupts" (
  "interrupt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt record */,
  "field_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration field associated with the interrupt */,
  "interrupt_description" TEXT /* Description of the interrupt behavior */,
  "interrupt_level" TEXT /* Level of the interrupt */,
  "interrupt_priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the interrupt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interrupt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupts_field_id FOREIGN KEY ("field_id") REFERENCES configuration_fields ("field_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "event_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "event_type_name" TEXT /* The name of the event type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "event_type_category" TEXT /* Category of the event type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Status indicating whether the notification has been read */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_category" TEXT /* Category of the setting */,
  "setting_subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
